_comment: forge-handoff.template.json — the hand-off the forge emits after scaffolding a born-conformant skill (A10 §3.7). It enumerates the seeded TODOs the author must fill to turn the contract-clean skeleton into a finished skill. The scaffolder produces a copy of this stub populated with the new skill's name and the resolved roster/components; the author works the `todos` list, then runs the A8 eval gate locally before pushing.

todos.what: Author the domain method: the analysis steps, the standards/figures it grounds in, and how each conclusion traces to evidence with a named owner and date.

Replace the seeded <!-- TODO: author this skill's intake question set --> with this skill's DOMAIN question VALUES. This is the §2.7 RUNTIME intake — categorically distinct from the forge's author-interview (A10 D4).
Refine the jurisdiction rows + the KB manifest for the jurisdictions/fragments this skill actually serves; every KB-... id must resolve in its folder's _registry.yaml (rule 9).
Author the skill's pre-output validation gate (every finding traced to evidence; named owner + date on every action; de-id pass complete before drafting).

Replace the seeded TODO cases with >=3 real cases (specificity + hierarchy-of-controls + a de_identification hard-fail case wiring evals/files/deid-leak.md). Run evals/run_evals.py locally (model-graded >=4.0, no hard-fail) before pushing.
Obtain the competent-person review + the SME-persona pass before merge (the durable quality lever).
